---
title: "Plots"
output: html_document
---


```{r }
# ==============================================================================
# Attrition Analysis using d_analysis (all available data)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Attrition by Survey Year (syear) ===\n")

# Calculate attrition rates by year
attrition_by_year <- d_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(attrition), !is.na(syear)) %>%
  group_by(syear) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_attrition = sum(attrition == 1),
    attrition_rate = mean(attrition) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(syear_num = as.numeric(as.character(syear)))

print(attrition_by_year, n = Inf)

# Plot: Line chart over time
p_year <- ggplot(attrition_by_year, aes(x = syear_num, y = attrition_rate)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5, color = "#E69F00") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#E69F00") +
  labs(
    title = "Attrition Rate by Year",
    subtitle = "Attrition over time (excluding deaths and year 2023)",
    x = "Survey Year",
    y = "Attrition Rate (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_year)

# ==============================================================================
# Attrition by Sample with Year Gradient (HEATMAP)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Attrition by Sample with Year Gradient ===\n")

# Calculate attrition by sample AND year for the gradient
attrition_sample_year <- d_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(attrition), !is.na(psample), !is.na(syear)) %>%
  group_by(psample, syear) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_attrition = sum(attrition == 1),
    attrition_rate = mean(attrition) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(syear_num = as.numeric(as.character(syear)))

# Calculate overall sample attrition for ordering
sample_order <- attrition_sample_year %>%
  group_by(psample) %>%
  summarise(overall_rate = mean(attrition_rate), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(overall_rate)

# Add ordering factor
attrition_sample_year <- attrition_sample_year %>%
  mutate(psample = factor(psample, levels = sample_order$psample))

# Plot: Heatmap (sample x year)
p_heatmap <- ggplot(attrition_sample_year, 
                     aes(x = syear_num, y = psample, fill = attrition_rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Attrition\nRate (%)",
    option = "plasma",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Attrition Rate by Sample Type and Year",
    subtitle = "Heatmap: darker colors = higher attrition",
    x = "Survey Year",
    y = "Sample (psample)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

print(p_heatmap)
```




```{r }
library(rstanarm)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

# ==============================================================================
# Setup: Complete cases and typical values
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Generating Posterior Predictions ===\n")

# Create complete case dataset
d_complete <- d_analysis %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(bmi_std), !is.na(grip_max), !is.na(attrition),
    !is.na(pf_nbs), !is.na(rp_nbs), !is.na(bp_nbs), !is.na(gh_nbs),
    !is.na(vt_nbs), !is.na(sf_nbs), !is.na(re_nbs), !is.na(mh_nbs),
    !is.na(age), !is.na(female), !is.na(employed), 
    !is.na(migback), !is.na(partner), !is.na(pgtatzeit), !is.na(pgbilzeit),
    !is.na(pgmode), !is.na(psample), !is.na(syear)
  )

cat("Complete cases:", nrow(d_complete), "\n")

# Get typical values
typical_values <- list(
  pf_nbs = median(d_complete$pf_nbs), rp_nbs = median(d_complete$rp_nbs),
  bp_nbs = median(d_complete$bp_nbs), gh_nbs = median(d_complete$gh_nbs),
  vt_nbs = median(d_complete$vt_nbs), sf_nbs = median(d_complete$sf_nbs),
  re_nbs = median(d_complete$re_nbs), mh_nbs = median(d_complete$mh_nbs),
  age = median(d_complete$age), grip_max = median(d_complete$grip_max),
  pgtatzeit = median(d_complete$pgtatzeit), pgbilzeit = median(d_complete$pgbilzeit),
  female = names(sort(table(d_complete$female), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
  partner = as.numeric(names(sort(table(d_complete$partner), decreasing = TRUE))[1]),
  employed = as.numeric(names(sort(table(d_complete$employed), decreasing = TRUE))[1]),
  migback = as.numeric(names(sort(table(d_complete$migback), decreasing = TRUE))[1]),
  pgmode = names(sort(table(d_complete$pgmode), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
  psample = names(sort(table(d_complete$psample), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
  syear = names(sort(table(d_complete$syear), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
)

# ==============================================================================
# Helper Functions
# ==============================================================================

create_pred_data <- function(var_values, var_name) {
  # Create base dataframe with typical values
  pred_data <- data.frame(typical_values, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  pred_data <- pred_data[rep(1, length(var_values)), , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Set the focal variable
  pred_data[[var_name]] <- var_values
  
  # Set bmi_std to 0 if not the focal variable
  if(var_name != "bmi_std") {
    pred_data$bmi_std <- 0
  }
  
  # Set migback to 0 if not the focal variable
  if(var_name != "migback") {
    pred_data$migback <- 0
  }
  
  # Convert factors
  pred_data$female <- factor(pred_data$female, levels = levels(d_complete$female))
  pred_data$pgmode <- factor(pred_data$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode)))
  pred_data$psample <- factor(pred_data$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample)))
  pred_data$syear <- factor(pred_data$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear)))
  
  return(pred_data)
}

summarize_predictions <- function(pp) {
  data.frame(
    Mean_Prob = colMeans(pp),
    SD = apply(pp, 2, sd),
    CI_Lower = apply(pp, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025),
    CI_Upper = apply(pp, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975)
  )
}

# ==============================================================================
# Categorical Variables (Binary & Multi-level)
# ==============================================================================

plot_categorical <- function(var_name, var_labels, plot_title, subtitle, color = "steelblue") {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s Effect ---\n", var_name))
  
  var_values <- if(var_name == "pgmode") {
    sort(unique(d_complete[[var_name]]))
  } else {
    c(0, 1)
  }
  
  # Special handling for pgmode to avoid replication issues
  if(var_name == "pgmode") {
    pred_data <- data.frame(
      pgmode = var_values,
      bmi_std = 0,
      grip_max = typical_values$grip_max,
      employed = typical_values$employed,
      migback = 0,
      pgtatzeit = typical_values$pgtatzeit,
      pgbilzeit = typical_values$pgbilzeit,
      pf_nbs = typical_values$pf_nbs,
      rp_nbs = typical_values$rp_nbs,
      bp_nbs = typical_values$bp_nbs,
      gh_nbs = typical_values$gh_nbs,
      vt_nbs = typical_values$vt_nbs,
      sf_nbs = typical_values$sf_nbs,
      re_nbs = typical_values$re_nbs,
      mh_nbs = typical_values$mh_nbs,
      age = typical_values$age,
      female = typical_values$female,
      partner = typical_values$partner,
      psample = typical_values$psample,
      syear = typical_values$syear,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    # Convert factors
    pred_data$female <- factor(pred_data$female, levels = levels(d_complete$female))
    pred_data$pgmode <- factor(pred_data$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode)))
    pred_data$psample <- factor(pred_data$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample)))
    pred_data$syear <- factor(pred_data$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear)))
  } else {
    pred_data <- create_pred_data(var_values, var_name)
  }
  
  pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
  summary_df <- summarize_predictions(pp)
  summary_df$Category <- var_labels
  
  cat("\nPredicted Attrition Probability:\n")
  print(summary_df, digits = 3)
  
  if(length(var_values) == 2) {
    diff <- pp[, 2] - pp[, 1]
    cat(sprintf("\nDifference: %.1f pp (95%% CI: %.1f to %.1f)\n",
                mean(diff) * 100, quantile(diff, 0.025) * 100, quantile(diff, 0.975) * 100))
  }
  
  # Use reorder for multi-level categorical variables
  if(length(var_values) > 2) {
    p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = reorder(Category, Mean_Prob), y = Mean_Prob * 100)) +
      geom_col(fill = color, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower * 100, ymax = CI_Upper * 100), width = 0.2) +
      labs(title = plot_title, subtitle = subtitle,
           y = "Attrition Probability (%)", x = "") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
      ) +
      coord_flip()
  } else {
    p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = Category, y = Mean_Prob * 100)) +
      geom_col(fill = color, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower * 100, ymax = CI_Upper * 100), width = 0.2) +
      labs(title = plot_title, subtitle = subtitle,
           y = "Attrition Probability (%)", x = "") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
      )
  }
  
  print(p)
  
  invisible(list(plot = p, summary = summary_df))
}

# Plot categorical variables
plot_categorical("migback", c("No", "Yes"), 
                "Predicted Attrition by Migration Background",
                "All other variables at typical values")

plot_categorical("employed", c("Unemployed", "Employed"),
                "Predicted Attrition by Employment Status",
                "Employed individuals less likely to attrit", "coral")

plot_categorical("pgmode", sort(unique(d_complete$pgmode)),
                "Predicted Attrition by Interview Mode",
                "Different survey modes have varying attrition rates", "tomato")

# ==============================================================================
# Continuous Variables (Ribbon Plots)
# ==============================================================================

plot_continuous <- function(var_name, x_label, plot_title, subtitle, color = "steelblue", 
                           quantile_range = NULL, full_range = FALSE) {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s Effect ---\n", var_name))
  
  if(!is.null(quantile_range)) {
    var_range <- seq(quantile(d_complete[[var_name]], quantile_range[1], na.rm = TRUE),
                    quantile(d_complete[[var_name]], quantile_range[2], na.rm = TRUE),
                    length.out = 50)
  } else if(full_range) {
    var_range <- seq(min(d_complete[[var_name]], na.rm = TRUE),
                    max(d_complete[[var_name]], na.rm = TRUE),
                    length.out = 50)
  } else {
    var_range <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = 50)  # For standardized vars
  }
  
  pred_data <- create_pred_data(var_range, var_name)
  pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
  summary_df <- summarize_predictions(pp)
  summary_df$X <- var_range
  
  p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = X, y = Mean_Prob * 100)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = CI_Lower * 100, ymax = CI_Upper * 100), 
                fill = color, alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_line(color = color, size = 1.2) +
    labs(title = plot_title, subtitle = subtitle,
         y = "Attrition Probability (%)", x = x_label) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
    )
  
  print(p)
  invisible(list(plot = p, summary = summary_df))
}

# Plot continuous variables
plot_continuous("bmi_std", "BMI (standardized)", 
               "Predicted Attrition by BMI",
               "Higher BMI → Lower attrition (protective effect)")

plot_continuous("grip_max", "Maximum Grip Strength (kg)",
               "Predicted Attrition by Grip Strength",
               "Higher grip strength → Lower attrition", "darkgreen",
               quantile_range = c(0.05, 0.95))

plot_continuous("age", "Age (years)",
               "Predicted Attrition by Age",
               "Older age → Higher attrition risk", "#E69F00", 
               full_range = TRUE)

plot_continuous("pgtatzeit", "Actual Work Hours per Week",
               "Predicted Attrition by Weekly Work Hours",
               "More Work per Week → Lower attrition", "purple", 
               full_range = TRUE)

plot_continuous("pgbilzeit", "Years of Education",
               "Predicted Attrition by Education",
               "More education → Lower attrition", "#cb94f7", 
               full_range = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
# SF-12 Health Subscales (Horizontal Bar Plot)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n--- SF-12 Health Subscales Effect ---\n")

health_vars <- c("pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", "gh_nbs", 
                 "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs")
health_labels <- c("Physical Functioning", "Role Physical", "Bodily Pain", 
                   "General Health", "Vitality", "Social Functioning",
                   "Role Emotional", "Mental Health")

health_results <- map2_dfr(health_vars, health_labels, function(var, label) {
  low_val <- quantile(d_complete[[var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  high_val <- quantile(d_complete[[var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  pred_low <- create_pred_data(low_val, var)
  pred_high <- create_pred_data(high_val, var)
  
  pp_low <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_low)
  pp_high <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_high)
  diff <- pp_high - pp_low
  
  data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    Diff = mean(diff) * 100,
    CI_Lower = quantile(diff, 0.025) * 100,
    CI_Upper = quantile(diff, 0.975) * 100,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

cat("\nHealth Subscale Effects (75th vs 25th percentile):\n")
print(health_results, digits = 2)

plot_health <- ggplot(health_results, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Diff), y = Diff)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "SF-12 Health Subscales Effect on Attrition",
       subtitle = "Change from 25th to 75th percentile",
       y = "Change in Attrition Probability (percentage points)", x = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
  )

print(plot_health)

# ==============================================================================
# Gender Effect (Female vs Male)
# ==============================================================================


cat("\n--- Female Effect ---\n")

# Get the actual factor levels for female
female_levels <- levels(d_complete$female)

# Create prediction data manually
pred_data <- data.frame(
  female = factor(female_levels, levels = female_levels),
  bmi_std = 0,
  grip_max = typical_values$grip_max,
  employed = typical_values$employed,
  migback = 0,
  pgtatzeit = typical_values$pgtatzeit,
  pgbilzeit = typical_values$pgbilzeit,
  pf_nbs = typical_values$pf_nbs,
  rp_nbs = typical_values$rp_nbs,
  bp_nbs = typical_values$bp_nbs,
  gh_nbs = typical_values$gh_nbs,
  vt_nbs = typical_values$vt_nbs,
  sf_nbs = typical_values$sf_nbs,
  re_nbs = typical_values$re_nbs,
  mh_nbs = typical_values$mh_nbs,
  age = typical_values$age,
  partner = typical_values$partner,
  pgmode = factor(typical_values$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode))),
  psample = factor(typical_values$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample))),
  syear = factor(typical_values$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear))),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
summary_df <- summarize_predictions(pp)
summary_df$Category <- c("Male", "Female")

cat("\nPredicted Attrition Probability:\n")
print(summary_df, digits = 3)

diff <- pp[, 2] - pp[, 1]
cat(sprintf("\nDifference (Female - Male): %.1f pp (95%% CI: %.1f to %.1f)\n",
            mean(diff) * 100, quantile(diff, 0.025) * 100, quantile(diff, 0.975) * 100))

plot_female <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = Category, y = Mean_Prob * 100)) +
  geom_col(fill = "coral", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower * 100, ymax = CI_Upper * 100), width = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "Predicted Attrition by Gender",
       subtitle = "All other variables at typical values",
       y = "Attrition Probability (%)", x = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
  )

print(plot_female)
```




```{r }
# ==============================================================================
# SF-12 Health Variables - Ribbon Plots
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n=== SF-12 Health Variables: Ribbon Plots ===\n")
health_vars <- c("pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", "gh_nbs", 
                 "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs")
health_labels <- c("Physical Functioning", "Role Physical", "Bodily Pain", 
                   "General Health", "Vitality", "Social Functioning",
                   "Role Emotional", "Mental Health")
# Function to create ribbon plot for health variables
plot_health_ribbon <- function(var_name, var_label, color = "coral") {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s Effect ---\n", var_label))
  
  # full, can be tweaked 
  var_range <- seq(quantile(d_complete[[var_name]], 0, na.rm = TRUE),
                   quantile(d_complete[[var_name]], 1, na.rm = TRUE),
                   length.out = 50)
  
  pred_data <- create_pred_data(var_range, var_name)
  pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
  summary_df <- summarize_predictions(pp)
  summary_df$X <- var_range
  
  # Calculate range of predictions for subtitle
  pred_range <- range(summary_df$Mean_Prob * 100)
  range_text <- sprintf("Prediction range: %.1f%% - %.1f%%", pred_range[1], pred_range[2])
  
  p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = X, y = Mean_Prob * 100)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = CI_Lower * 100, ymax = CI_Upper * 100), 
                fill = color, alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_line(color = color, size = 1.2) +
    labs(title = paste("Predicted Attrition by", var_label),
         subtitle = range_text,
         y = "Attrition Probability (%)", 
         x = paste(var_label, "(norm-based score)")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
    )
  
  print(p)
  
  # Print summary
  cat(sprintf("Range: %.1f to %.1f\n", min(var_range), max(var_range)))
  cat(sprintf("Prediction range: %.1f%% to %.1f%%\n", pred_range[1], pred_range[2]))
  cat(sprintf("Total change: %.2f percentage points\n", pred_range[2] - pred_range[1]))
  
  invisible(list(plot = p, summary = summary_df))
}
# Create ribbon plots for all health variables
health_ribbon_plots <- map2(health_vars, health_labels, function(var, label) {
  plot_health_ribbon(var, label)
})
# Optional: Create a summary comparison showing the slope/change for each variable
health_slopes <- map2_dfr(health_vars, health_labels, function(var, label) {
  var_range <- seq(quantile(d_complete[[var]], 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
                   quantile(d_complete[[var]], 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
                   length.out = 50)
  
  pred_data <- create_pred_data(var_range, var_name = var)
  pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
  
  # Calculate change from lowest to highest
  change <- (pp[, 50] - pp[, 1]) * 100  # percentage points
  
  data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    Mean_Change = mean(change),
    CI_Lower = quantile(change, 0.025),
    CI_Upper = quantile(change, 0.975),
    Significant = !(quantile(change, 0.025) < 0 & quantile(change, 0.975) > 0)
  )
})
cat("\n=== Summary: Change from 5th to 95th Percentile ===\n")
print(health_slopes %>% arrange(desc(abs(Mean_Change))), digits = 2)
# Plot the summary
ggplot(health_slopes, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Mean_Change), y = Mean_Change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper,
                      color = Significant), 
                  size = 0.8, fatten = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey60", "coral")) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "SF-12 Health Variables: Effect Sizes",
       subtitle = "Change in attrition from 5th to 95th percentile (95% CrI)",
       x = "",
       y = "Change in Attrition Probability (percentage points)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
  )
```




```{r }
# ==============================================================================
# Mental Health Effect by Interview Mode 
# ==============================================================================

library(rstanarm)
library(tidyverse)

cat("\n=== Mental Health × Interview Mode Interaction ===\n")

# Get all interview modes
interview_modes <- sort(unique(d_complete$pgmode))
cat("Interview modes:", paste(interview_modes, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Mental health range (5th to 95th percentile)
mh_range <- seq(quantile(d_complete$mh_nbs, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d_complete$mh_nbs, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 50)

# Generate predictions for each mode
mode_predictions <- map_dfr(interview_modes, function(mode) {
  cat(sprintf("\nProcessing mode: %s\n", mode))
  
  # Create prediction data for this mode
  pred_data <- data.frame(
    mh_nbs = mh_range,
    pgmode = mode,
    bmi_std = 0,
    grip_max = typical_values$grip_max,
    employed = typical_values$employed,
    migback = 0,
    pgtatzeit = typical_values$pgtatzeit,
    pgbilzeit = typical_values$pgbilzeit,
    pf_nbs = typical_values$pf_nbs,
    rp_nbs = typical_values$rp_nbs,
    bp_nbs = typical_values$bp_nbs,
    gh_nbs = typical_values$gh_nbs,
    vt_nbs = typical_values$vt_nbs,
    sf_nbs = typical_values$sf_nbs,
    re_nbs = typical_values$re_nbs,
    age = typical_values$age,
    female = typical_values$female,
    partner = typical_values$partner,
    psample = typical_values$psample,
    syear = typical_values$syear,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Convert factors
  pred_data$female <- factor(pred_data$female, levels = levels(d_complete$female))
  pred_data$pgmode <- factor(pred_data$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode)))
  pred_data$psample <- factor(pred_data$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample)))
  pred_data$syear <- factor(pred_data$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear)))
  
  # Get predictions
  pp <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_data)
  
  # Summarize
  data.frame(
    Mode = mode,
    MH_Score = mh_range,
    Mean_Prob = colMeans(pp) * 100,
    CI_Lower = apply(pp, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025) * 100,
    CI_Upper = apply(pp, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975) * 100
  )
})

# Print summary statistics by mode
cat("\n=== Summary by Mode ===\n")
mode_summary <- mode_predictions %>%
  group_by(Mode) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    Max_Prob = max(Mean_Prob),
    Min_Prob = min(Mean_Prob),
    Range = Max_Prob - Min_Prob,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Max_Prob))

print(mode_summary, digits = 2)

# Calculate average attrition rate by interview mode
mode_attrition <- d_complete %>%
  group_by(pgmode) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    Avg_Attrition = mean(attrition, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Average Attrition by Interview Mode ===\n")
print(mode_attrition, digits = 2)

# Create individual plots for each mode
cat("\n=== Creating Individual Plots for Each Mode ===\n")

for(mode in interview_modes) {
  mode_data <- mode_predictions %>% filter(Mode == mode)
  mode_avg <- mode_attrition %>% filter(pgmode == mode) %>% pull(Avg_Attrition)
  
  # Calculate range of effect for this mode
  effect_range <- max(mode_data$Mean_Prob) - min(mode_data$Mean_Prob)
  
  p <- ggplot(mode_data, aes(x = MH_Score, y = Mean_Prob)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mode_avg, linetype = "dashed", 
               color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), 
                fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_line(color = "#56B4E9", linewidth = 1.2) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 20)) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Mental Health Effect on Attrition -", mode),
      subtitle = sprintf("95%% credible intervals | Mode avg = %.1f%% | Effect range = %.1f%%", 
                        mode_avg, effect_range),
      x = "Mental Health Score (MH - norm-based)",
      y = "Attrition Probability (%)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 13)
    )
  
  print(p)
  cat("\n")
}

cat("\n=== Analysis Complete ===\n")
cat("Individual plot created for each interview mode\n")
cat("All plots use same y-axis (0-20%) for direct comparison\n")
cat("Red dashed line shows average attrition for that specific interview mode\n")

```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Mental Health Effect Range by Interview Mode
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Mental Health Effect Range by Interview Mode ===\n")

# Get all interview modes
interview_modes <- sort(unique(d_complete$pgmode))

# Mental health range (0th to 100th percentile) - FULL RANGE
mh_low <- quantile(d_complete$mh_nbs, 0, na.rm = TRUE)
mh_high <- quantile(d_complete$mh_nbs, 1, na.rm = TRUE)

# Calculate effect range for each interview mode
mode_effects <- map_dfr(interview_modes, function(mode) {
  cat(sprintf("\nProcessing mode: %s\n", mode))
  
  # Create prediction data for LOW mental health
  pred_low <- data.frame(
    mh_nbs = mh_low,
    pgmode = mode,
    bmi_std = 0,
    grip_max = typical_values$grip_max,
    employed = typical_values$employed,
    migback = 0,
    pgtatzeit = typical_values$pgtatzeit,
    pgbilzeit = typical_values$pgbilzeit,
    pf_nbs = typical_values$pf_nbs,
    rp_nbs = typical_values$rp_nbs,
    bp_nbs = typical_values$bp_nbs,
    gh_nbs = typical_values$gh_nbs,
    vt_nbs = typical_values$vt_nbs,
    sf_nbs = typical_values$sf_nbs,
    re_nbs = typical_values$re_nbs,
    age = typical_values$age,
    female = typical_values$female,
    partner = typical_values$partner,
    psample = typical_values$psample,
    syear = typical_values$syear,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Create prediction data for HIGH mental health
  pred_high <- data.frame(
    mh_nbs = mh_high,
    pgmode = mode,
    bmi_std = 0,
    grip_max = typical_values$grip_max,
    employed = typical_values$employed,
    migback = 0,
    pgtatzeit = typical_values$pgtatzeit,
    pgbilzeit = typical_values$pgbilzeit,
    pf_nbs = typical_values$pf_nbs,
    rp_nbs = typical_values$rp_nbs,
    bp_nbs = typical_values$bp_nbs,
    gh_nbs = typical_values$gh_nbs,
    vt_nbs = typical_values$vt_nbs,
    sf_nbs = typical_values$sf_nbs,
    re_nbs = typical_values$re_nbs,
    age = typical_values$age,
    female = typical_values$female,
    partner = typical_values$partner,
    psample = typical_values$psample,
    syear = typical_values$syear,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Convert factors
  pred_low$female <- factor(pred_low$female, levels = levels(d_complete$female))
  pred_low$pgmode <- factor(pred_low$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode)))
  pred_low$psample <- factor(pred_low$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample)))
  pred_low$syear <- factor(pred_low$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear)))
  
  pred_high$female <- factor(pred_high$female, levels = levels(d_complete$female))
  pred_high$pgmode <- factor(pred_high$pgmode, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$pgmode)))
  pred_high$psample <- factor(pred_high$psample, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$psample)))
  pred_high$syear <- factor(pred_high$syear, levels = levels(factor(d_complete$syear)))
  
  # Get predictions
  pp_low <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_low)
  pp_high <- posterior_epred(model4_bayes, newdata = pred_high)
  
  # Calculate difference (effect of going from low to high MH)
  diff <- (pp_high - pp_low) * 100  # percentage points
  
  data.frame(
    Mode = mode,
    MH_Effect = mean(diff),
    CI_Lower = quantile(diff, 0.025),
    CI_Upper = quantile(diff, 0.975),
    Prob_Low = mean(pp_low) * 100,
    Prob_High = mean(pp_high) * 100
  )
})

cat("\n=== Mental Health Effect by Interview Mode ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Mental Health range: %.1f (min) to %.1f (max)\n", mh_low, mh_high))
print(mode_effects, digits = 2)

# Create the plot - FLIPPED ORDER (reverse the reorder)
plot_mh_mode <- ggplot(mode_effects, aes(x = reorder(Mode, -MH_Effect), y = MH_Effect)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Mental Health Effect Range by Interview Mode",
    y = "Change in Attrition Probability (percentage points)",
    x = "Interview Mode"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(plot_mh_mode)



cat("\n=== Interpretation ===\n")
cat("The first plot shows the EFFECT SIZE of mental health for each interview mode\n")
cat("  - Negative values = better mental health reduces attrition\n")
cat("  - Larger absolute values = stronger mental health effect\n\n")


```




```{r }
# ==============================================================================
# Interview Mode Distribution Over Time
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Interview Mode Distribution Over Time ===\n")

# Define the specific order for interview modes 
mode_numbers <- c(170, 150, 131, 140, 132, 230, 220, 110, 120, 160, 210)

# Calculate interview mode distribution by year
mode_by_year <- d_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(pgmode), !is.na(syear)) %>%
  # Extract just the number from pgmode
  mutate(pgmode_num = as.numeric(str_extract(pgmode, "\\d+"))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pgmode_num)) %>%
  # Only keep modes in our specified list
  filter(pgmode_num %in% mode_numbers) %>%
  group_by(syear, pgmode_num) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(syear) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percentage = (n / total) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    syear_num = as.numeric(as.character(syear)),
    pgmode_num = factor(pgmode_num, levels = mode_numbers)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pgmode_num))  # Remove any NA factors

# Get overall mode frequencies for summary
mode_order_overall <- mode_by_year %>%
  group_by(pgmode_num) %>%
  summarise(total_n = sum(n), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(match(pgmode_num, mode_numbers))

cat("\nInterview mode coverage:\n")
print(mode_order_overall)

# Plot 3: Heatmap of interview mode by year
p_mode_heatmap <- ggplot(mode_by_year, 
                         aes(x = syear_num, y = pgmode_num, fill = percentage)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Percentage\n(%)",
    option = "plasma",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Interview Mode Distribution: Heatmap by Year",
    subtitle = "Percentage of each interview mode over time",
    x = "Survey Year",
    y = "Interview Mode"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

print(p_mode_heatmap)
```
